title: Lesson 66
paragraph: This lesson practices useful nouns for meals and table items.

words.meaning: a list of food in a restaurant
paper or cloth used while eating
words.example: Put the napkin on your lap.

words.meaning: a small tube used for drinking
words.example: She drinks juice with a straw.

words.meaning: a flat thing used to carry food or cups
words.example: Put your lunch on the tray.

words.meaning: one of two thin sticks used for eating
a pot used for making or pouring tea
words.example: The teapot is on the table.

words.meaning: a thing used to boil water
a very cold box for keeping food frozen
words.example: The ice cream is in the freezer.

words.meaning: food that remains after a meal
a place to eat in a school or company
words.example: Students eat lunch in the cafeteria.
